audits.cleanReason: Checked the only live negation-shape candidates that could plausibly read as pivots. The surviving lines are either procedural commands or hard capability/mechanism limits, so no sentence depended on negation scaffolding for its meaning.

findings.text: Whatever silver had paid for this border, it had not stretched far enough south.
findings.subject: silver
findings.realAgent: the people or state authority that funded and limited the ward line
findings.reason: An inanimate resource is doing the paying and failing instead of the actual funders or the border network.
findings.suggestedFix: For what it had cost, the border ended too far north.
notes: Appears inside a texture-desert zone flagged by rhythm_scorer.

findings.text: make her hands want to shake
findings.subject: hands
findings.realAgent: Jaina, reacting physically to the contamination pressure
findings.reason: The phrase gives Jaina's hands independent desire instead of stating her own involuntary reaction.
notes: Appears inside the same road-reading texture-desert cluster.

findings.text: Jaina's hands stopped halfway through the explanation she had been about to give.
findings.subject: hands
findings.realAgent: Jaina, cutting herself off mid-gesture when Sylvanas outpaces her explanation
findings.reason: The body-part subject steals the interruption from Jaina herself.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina cut herself off halfway through the explanation she had been about to give.
notes: Sits in the same analytical-P0V cluster the guide marks as high risk.

findings.text: the whole road narrowing to wolves, hooves, and the taste of copper in her mouth
findings.subject: road
findings.reason: The road is made to perform Jaina's tunnel vision instead of the narration naming her perception directly.
findings.suggestedFix: wolves, hooves, and the taste of copper in her mouth crowding out everything else

findings.text: The formation had pulled tight now.
findings.subject: formation
findings.realAgent: the escort riders closing ranks around Jaina
findings.reason: An abstract arrangement is assigned the riders' physical action.
findings.suggestedFix: The riders had pulled in tight now.

audits.cleanReason: Audited every pass-A slop-checker line mapped to recurring tics plus the guide's chapter caps. No tic exceeded its chapter limit: filter-word signals stayed sparse, the five similes reached but did not exceed the chapter cap, participial attachments stayed well under cap, punctuation-tic colons stopped at cap, and the chapter did not overrun any listed tic family once tactical looks and literal physical uses were filtered out.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A still has 5 mild intent-smear kills in v2-r4.md, including a clustered road-reading section where agency keeps slipping onto silver, hands, and scene geometry. More than 3 mild-only kills means the pass fails.

textureMetrics.interpretation: PARTICIPIAL PHRASES: 2.4% (floor: 5.0%, human: 11.0%). Add ', Ving' phrases to connect actions: ', turning back to the fire' / ', gripping the railing with both hands'. These create connective tissue between actions. | SEMICOLONS: 0.0% (floor: 0.3%, human: 1.4%). Join related clauses: 'She didn't answer; the question wasn't meant for her.'
